campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. The council also discussed extending a temporary local rule and moving forward with plans for a bike and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donating $4,000 for youth scholarships
Extending temporary local ordinance #2326
Planning and funding a bicycle/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for Hamilton Avenue crossing improvements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council met to decide which organizations and events would receive grant money from the city. They approved funding for specific groups that had applied for grants, set aside money for competitive event grants in the upcoming budget year, and made some rule changes to clarify how community events and street closures are handled.
keyTopics: Approved grant recipients and their individual funding amounts
Set up Competitive Event Grant Program budget for the fiscal year
Updated rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grants

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple channels including cable TV, online streaming, and phone call-in options. Live translations in over 50 languages were available for those who needed them.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (Cable Channel 26, YouTube, Zoom, or phone)
Live translation services available in 50+ languages
Meeting accessible online at sanjoseca.gov and YouTube
Phone dial-in option for residents without internet access

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026 to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. Residents had the opportunity to speak during public comment time, and council members discussed various committee reports and upcoming board meetings.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Schedules for upcoming board and commission meetings

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale. The commission approved previous meeting notes and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding to support local housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Federal grant applications for affordable housing projects
Community development funding opportunities
Human services program funding requests
Approval of February 25 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses succeed by launching a program that forgives unpaid business license fees for companies that get current, reaching out to shopping centers to spread the word. They also discussed plans to support small businesses and startups through incentives, promote local shopping through summer events, and partner with private companies to sponsor city events.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - forgive back fees if businesses catch up
Shopping center outreach to help retailers and mall tenants
Summer Shop Local Campaign to encourage residents to buy locally
Economic incentives for small businesses and startups
Public-Private Partnerships for city event sponsorships

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara Governance and Ethics Committee — March 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's city leadership is holding a Governance and Ethics Committee meeting on March 30, 2026. Residents can attend in person or join remotely via Zoom or phone, and can submit written comments by email before the meeting.
keyTopics: Governance and Ethics Committee meeting scheduled
In-person and remote participation options available
Public comment submission deadline: 10 a.m. one day before meeting
Zoom access and phone dial-in information provided for residents

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to recognize Women's History Month and approve two agreements. The council signed off on a disaster-response facility agreement with the American Red Cross and authorized a $93,383 annual purchase for email security software (Mimecast) to protect city computer systems.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross facility agreement for disaster response
Mimecast email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
